title: Martin Amis: An Appreciation
summary: Our critic assesses the achievement of Martin Amis, Britain’s most famous literary son.

title: Book Review: ‘NB by J.C.,’ by James Campbell
summary: “NB by J.C.” collects the variegated musings of James Campbell in the Times Literary Supplement.

title: In ‘Fires in the Dark,’ Kay Redfield Jamison Turns to Healers
summary: In “Fires in the Dark,” Jamison, known for her expertise on manic depression, delves into the quest to heal. Her new book, she says, is a “love song to psychotherapy.”

title: The Detective Novel ‘Whose Body?,’ by Dorothy L. Sayers, Turns 100
summary: Dorothy L. Sayers dealt with emotional and financial instability by writing “Whose Body?,” the first of many to star the detective Lord Peter Wimsey.

title: Book Review: ‘Dom Casmurro,’ by Machado de Assis
summary: “Dom Casmurro,” by Machado de Assis, teaches us to read — and reread — with precise detail and masterly obfuscation.

title: Book Review: ‘The Late Americans,’ by Brandon Taylor
summary: Brandon Taylor’s novel circulates among Iowa City residents, some privileged, some not, but all aware that their possibilities are contracting.

title: Martin Amis’s Best Books: A Guide
summary: The acclaimed British novelist was also an essayist, memoirist and critic of the first rank.

title: The Best Romance Novels of 2024 (So Far)
summary: Looking for an escapist love story? Here are 2024’s sexiest, swooniest reads.

title: What Book Should You Read Next?
summary: Finding a book you’ll love can be daunting. Let us help.

title: ‘Beaches’ to Close on Broadway Months Earlier Than Planned
summary: A musical version of the 1980s tear-jerker will close months earlier than planned after opening in April to negative reviews and soft sales.

title: International Booker Prize 2026: ‘Taiwan Travelogue’ is First Winner Originally Written in Mandarin
summary: “Taiwan Travelogue” is the first novel originally written in Mandarin to win the major award for fiction translated into English.

title: Broadway’s ‘Giant,’ With John Lithgow as Roald Dahl, Turns a Profit in 10 Weeks
summary: The fast success of this play, about the children’s author Roald Dahl, is a rarity on Broadway, where most shows lose money.

title: Book Review: ‘An Inconvenient Widow,’ by Lois Romano
summary: In “An Inconvenient Widow,” Lois Romano defends the most reviled first lady from her detractors past and present.

title: Do You Even Thinketh, Bro?
summary: Lots of guys are recommending “As a Man Thinketh” for self-improvement. That’s not what it gave me.

title: Book Review: ‘Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young,’ by Zayd Ayers Dohrn
summary: Zayd Ayers Dohrn’s parents were leaders of the Weather Underground. His new book traces how their revolutionary ideals collided with their family life.

title: Book Review: ‘Talking Classics,’ by Mary Beard
summary: For her latest book, the popular British scholar Mary Beard gets personal about how she fell for ancient Greece and Rome.

title: Book Review: ‘On Witness and Respair,’ by Jesmyn Ward
summary: The two-time National Book Award winner collects essays, profiles and appreciations in a new book, “On Witness and Respair.”

title: Book Review: ‘The Theater,’ by James Verini
summary: In “The Theater,” the journalist James Verini recounts the bombing of a performing arts space turned refugee shelter in the middle of war-torn Mariupol.

title: Best Enemies-to-Lovers Romance Books, According to Emily Henry
summary: The best-selling author recommends books by Sally Thorne, Jasmine Guillory and more where the line between nemeses and soul mates becomes deliciously flimsy.

title: Book Review: ‘How to Rule the World,’ by Theo Baker
summary: In his first book, Theo Baker chronicles an outrageously eventful year navigating an enigmatic center of power.

title: Kennedy Ryan on ‘Score,’ Her TV Deal, and Finding Purpose
summary: Kennedy Ryan just landed a TV deal for her breakout novel. Her new books challenge readers — and her characters — to dive into the Harlem Renaissance.

title: The Creator of ‘Shrek!’ Never Got to See the Memes
summary: The writer and illustrator William Steig lived several lives before his book about a surly ogre became a Hollywood hit.

title: Book Review: ‘The Wonderful World That Almost Was: A Life of Peter Hujar and Paul Thek’
summary: In “The Wonderful World That Almost Was,” Andrew Durbin reconstructs the coterie that surrounded the artist-lovers Peter Hujar and Paul Thek.

title: In a New Memoir, Hayden Panettiere Relives the Drama
summary: Her mother put her on TV at 11 months old. She lost custody of her own daughter. The “Nashville” star has a lot to reckon with in her new memoir.

title: Book Review: ‘Take Me With You,’ by Steven Rowley
summary: Steven Rowley’s new novel, “Take Me With You,” asks who we are without our partners.

title: Book Review: ‘Take Me to Your Leader,’ by Neil deGrasse Tyson
summary: The popular astrophysicist can be witty and informative when making science accessible. You wouldn’t know it from his new book, “Take Me to Your Leader.”

title: Book Review: ‘Attention-Seeking Behavior,’ by Aea Varfis-van Warmelo
summary: Aea Varfis-van Warmelo’s novel follows a commitment-averse liar who is plagued by visions of violence.

title: Archaeologists Find Egyptian Mummy Buried With the ‘Iliad’
summary: For the deceased of Roman-era Egypt, Greek literature may have offered a cheat code to a more comfortable afterlife.

title: The Author of the InvestiGators Graphic Novel Series Shares His Favorite Animal Comics
summary: The author of the InvestiGators series recommends books from his childhood as well as more recent faves.

title: Speculative Fiction Books Full of Real Horrors
summary: The best-selling author Vaishnavi Patel recommends speculative fiction by N.K. Jemisin, Olivie Blake and more that speaks to our world today.
